name: Vatstack API Vocabulary
description: Terms and concepts used across the Vatstack VAT validation API

terms.description: ISO date at which the object was created.
Unique identifier for the object.
Descriptive name of the object.
Array of all queries in the order of your input.
Array of all queries which are of invalid format. Ignored queries are automatically identified upon your input. Its items will not be validated.
ISO date at which the object status was changed to `scheduled`. Defaults to `null` while the status is `pending`.
The number of `validations` where `valid` is `true` or `false`. It’s an indicator for how far the batch has progressed.
ISO date at which the object was updated.

Array of the first 20 validation objects in the order of `queries`. If an `id` is shown in the object, it was created for the batch and you can retrieve it individually. See [validation object](https://vatstack.com/docs/validations) for reference.
For validations, a short string indicating the [error code](https://vatstack.com/docs/validations).
Human-readable message providing more details about the error.
Required pieces of evidence according to your account’s regional settings.
Category of the digital product. Defaults to `null` if no category was specified in the request or if the category cannot be applied for the `country_code`.
2-letter ISO country code.
Corresponding English name of `country_code`.
Localized name of the VAT identification number.
Boolean indicating whether `country_code` is an EU member state.
Abbreviation of `local_name`.
3-letter ISO 4217 local currency code.
3-letter ISO 4217 local currency code.
Standard VAT rate in percent.
Abbreviation of `vat_local_name`.
Localized name of the VAT.
An arbitrary string to describe the supplied item. Often useful for displaying to users.

Populated evidence object if an ID is attached. You can attach an evidence object with the `evidence` body parameter in the POST request. Defaults to `null`. See [evidence object](https://vatstack.com/docs/evidences) for reference.
Status of whether the attached evidence object sufficiently proves the place of supply established in `country_code`. Will be either `sufficient` or `insufficient`.
A custom string for the invoice number issued to the customer. It’s advisable to follow sequential numbering.
ISO date at which `invoice_number` was issued.
A custom string for additional notes.
Boolean indicating whether the company exists and is active. Use `valid` to check whether the business is also VAT-registered.
Address of the company the VAT number is associated with. Servers of Germany and Spain won’t return a value for privacy reasons and will default to `null`.
Name of the company the VAT number is associated with. Servers of Germany and Spain won’t return a value for privacy reasons and will default to `null`.
Type of the company entity returned by the respective government service (where available).

If you save your own VAT number in your dashboard, the reply will contain a unique consultation number. The consultation number enables you to prove to a tax administration of a Member State that you have checked a VAT number at the `requested` date, and obtained a validation result.
Your original query.
ISO date at which the validation request was originally performed. Types `eu_vat` and `gb_vat` do not specify a time.
Type of VAT number.
Boolean indicating whether the vat_number is registered for VAT. If government services are down, the value will be `null` and re-checked automatically for you.
Boolean indicating whether the VAT number contained in `query` is in a valid format.
VAT number extracted from your query without the country code.
A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 20.
Integer for the current page.
Show only objects with the given `status`.
Show only objects where the `issued` date is this date or later. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.
Show only objects where the `issued` date is this date or earlier. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.
Show only objects that belong to a batch with the given identifier.
Show only objects where the `requested` date is this date or later. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.
Show only objects where the `requested` date is this date or earlier. Format `YYYY-MM-DD`.
